name: Rock Cakes
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Cold butter, cut into small pieces
1/4 cup Solid vegetable shortening, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions: A staple at English boarding schools, these individual cakes are served with afternoon tea. They don't keep well; after a day they take on the characteristics of "rocks". Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C). Grease a large baking sheet and set aside. Sift flour, baking powder, salt, nutmeg, cinnamon, and cloves into a large bowl. With your fingers, rub in butter and shortening until the mixture is crumbly. Stir in raisins, walnuts, brown sugar, and lemon peel. Add egg and just enough milk to make a stiff dough. Drop tablespoonfuls of dough about 2 inches apart on the baking sheet. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes or until golden brown. Transfer to a wire rack to cool. Serve fresh. Makes about 15 rock cakes.
